context: Tests whether the agent applies correct filter ordering (line filter before parser) and selects the most appropriate parser for a semi-structured nginx access log. Both the log-filter query and metric query should demonstrate these principles.

checklist.name: Line filter before parser
checklist.description: The 5xx log-filter query places a line filter (e.g. |= "50" or |~ "5[0-9]{2}") BEFORE the parser stage — not after it

checklist.name: Pattern parser used
checklist.description: Uses the pattern parser (| pattern "...") to extract fields from the nginx access log format rather than regexp or json

checklist.name: Specific stream selector
checklist.description: Stream selector includes at least two label matchers (e.g. job and namespace, or job and cluster) rather than a single broad label

checklist.name: Status code via label filter
checklist.description: After parsing, the status code is filtered via a label filter (e.g. status >= 500) rather than only relying on the line filter

checklist.name: rate() for metric query
checklist.description: The metric (rate) query uses rate() not count_over_time()

checklist.name: sum by method grouping
checklist.description: The metric query aggregates using sum by (method) or equivalent grouping on the extracted HTTP method label

checklist.name: Customization notes present
checklist.description: The output document includes notes identifying which labels or values to change (e.g. namespace, cluster, time window)

checklist.name: Query explanations present
checklist.description: Each query is accompanied by a plain-English explanation of what it returns and how to interpret results
